title: chrysothamnus viscidiflorus var. lanceolatus
intro: Chrysothamnus viscidiflorus var. lanceolatus, also known as Bigelow's sticky chamisa, is a shrub-like plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant contains sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Chrysothamnus viscidiflorus var. lanceolatus include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to boost the immune system and protect against infections. Chrysothamnus viscidiflorus var. lanceolatus can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, making it a versatile and accessible remedy for various health issues.
